instruction: Implement an async TCP server using Boost.Asio with connection management, coroutine-based handlers, graceful shutdown, and proper lifetime management of shared state.
output:
<think>
Boost.Asio's coroutine support (C++20 coroutines via asio::awaitable) dramatically simplifies async network code compared to callback chains. Key design concerns: (1) Lifetime management is the biggest trap - when a connection handler coroutine is suspended, the objects it references must stay alive. Using shared_ptr<Session> and passing it to each coroutine ensures the session outlives its operations. (2) Connection management needs a central registry to track active connections for graceful shutdown. An unordered_set<shared_ptr<Session>> protected by a strand (or the implicit strand of the executor). (3) Graceful shutdown: stop accepting, signal all sessions to close, wait with timeout. Use a signal_set for SIGTERM/SIGINT, then cancel all pending operations. (4) Strand-per-connection ensures no concurrent handler execution for a single connection, avoiding data races without explicit locking. In Asio, co_spawn on a strand gives this automatically. (5) Error handling in coroutines: wrap operations in try/catch, distinguish between expected errors (connection reset, EOF) and real errors.
</think>

```cpp
#include <boost/asio.hpp>
#include <boost/asio/co_spawn.hpp>
#include <boost/asio/detached.hpp>
#include <boost/asio/signal_set.hpp>
#include <boost/asio/steady_timer.hpp>
#include <iostream>
#include <memory>
#include <set>
#include <mutex>

namespace asio = boost::asio;
using tcp = asio::ip::tcp;
using asio::awaitable;
using asio::co_spawn;
using asio::use_awaitable;

class Session : public std::enable_shared_from_this<Session> {
public:
    Session(tcp::socket socket, std::function<void(Session*)> on_close)
        : socket_(std::move(socket))
        , timer_(socket_.get_executor())
        , on_close_(std::move(on_close)) {
        peer_ = socket_.remote_endpoint().address().to_string()
              + ":" + std::to_string(socket_.remote_endpoint().port());
    }

    awaitable<void> run() {
        try {
            std::cout << "[+] Connected: " << peer_ << std::endl;
            char buf[4096];
            for (;;) {
                // Read with timeout
                timer_.expires_after(std::chrono::seconds(30));

                auto [ec_timer] = co_await asio::experimental::make_parallel_group(
                    timer_.async_wait(asio::as_tuple(use_awaitable)),
                    asio::async_read_some(socket_, asio::buffer(buf), asio::as_tuple(use_awaitable))
                ).async_wait(
                    asio::experimental::wait_for_one(),
                    use_awaitable
                );
                // Simplified: just do a normal read with cancellation
                auto n = co_await socket_.async_read_some(
                    asio::buffer(buf), use_awaitable);
                if (n == 0) break;

                // Echo back
                co_await asio::async_write(
                    socket_, asio::buffer(buf, n), use_awaitable);
                bytes_transferred_ += n;
            }
        } catch (const boost::system::system_error& e) {
            if (e.code() != asio::error::eof &&
                e.code() != asio::error::connection_reset &&
                e.code() != asio::error::operation_aborted) {
                std::cerr << "[-] Error from " << peer_ << ": " << e.what() << std::endl;
            }
        }
        std::cout << "[-] Disconnected: " << peer_
                  << " (" << bytes_transferred_ << " bytes)" << std::endl;
        on_close_(this);
    }

    void close() {
        boost::system::error_code ec;
        socket_.shutdown(tcp::socket::shutdown_both, ec);
        socket_.close(ec);
    }

    const std::string& peer() const { return peer_; }

private:
    tcp::socket socket_;
    asio::steady_timer timer_;
    std::function<void(Session*)> on_close_;
    std::string peer_;
    size_t bytes_transferred_ = 0;
};

class Server {
public:
    Server(asio::io_context& ctx, uint16_t port)
        : ctx_(ctx)
        , acceptor_(ctx, tcp::endpoint(tcp::v4(), port)) {
        std::cout << "Server listening on port " << port << std::endl;
    }

    awaitable<void> accept_loop() {
        while (acceptor_.is_open()) {
            try {
                auto socket = co_await acceptor_.async_accept(use_awaitable);
                auto session = std::make_shared<Session>(
                    std::move(socket),
                    [this](Session* s) { remove_session(s); }
                );
                {
                    std::lock_guard lock(sessions_mutex_);
                    sessions_.insert(session);
                }
                co_spawn(ctx_, session->run(), asio::detached);
            } catch (const boost::system::system_error& e) {
                if (e.code() == asio::error::operation_aborted) break;
                std::cerr << "Accept error: " << e.what() << std::endl;
            }
        }
    }

    void shutdown() {
        std::cout << "Shutting down server..." << std::endl;
        acceptor_.close();
        std::lock_guard lock(sessions_mutex_);
        for (auto& session : sessions_) {
            session->close();
        }
        std::cout << "Closed " << sessions_.size() << " connections" << std::endl;
    }

    size_t connection_count() const {
        std::lock_guard lock(sessions_mutex_);
        return sessions_.size();
    }

private:
    void remove_session(Session* raw) {
        std::lock_guard lock(sessions_mutex_);
        for (auto it = sessions_.begin(); it != sessions_.end(); ++it) {
            if (it->get() == raw) {
                sessions_.erase(it);
                break;
            }
        }
    }

    asio::io_context& ctx_;
    tcp::acceptor acceptor_;
    std::set<std::shared_ptr<Session>> sessions_;
    mutable std::mutex sessions_mutex_;
};

int main() {
    asio::io_context ctx;
    Server server(ctx, 8080);

    // Signal handling for graceful shutdown
    asio::signal_set signals(ctx, SIGINT, SIGTERM);
    signals.async_wait([&](auto, auto) {
        server.shutdown();
    });

    co_spawn(ctx, server.accept_loop(), asio::detached);
    ctx.run();
    return 0;
}
```

instruction: Write a C++ non-blocking I/O multiplexer using epoll on Linux that handles thousands of concurrent connections with edge-triggered events and proper buffer management.
output:
<think>
Epoll with edge-triggered (ET) mode is the foundation of high-performance Linux servers. Key differences from level-triggered: ET only notifies once when state changes, so we MUST drain the entire buffer on EPOLLIN or we'll miss data. This means: read in a loop until EAGAIN. Level-triggered re-notifies as long as there's data, which is simpler but generates more syscalls. For thousands of connections: (1) Use EPOLL_CTL_ADD with EPOLLIN | EPOLLET | EPOLLONESHOT for safety - ONESHOT means we must re-arm after each event, preventing multiple threads from handling the same fd simultaneously. (2) Buffer management - each connection needs a read buffer and a write buffer. We use a per-connection struct with dynamic buffers. (3) Non-blocking sockets are mandatory with ET - set O_NONBLOCK on every accepted socket. (4) Write handling: when we can't write everything (EAGAIN), enable EPOLLOUT and buffer the remainder. When EPOLLOUT fires, flush the buffer and disable EPOLLOUT. (5) Connection lifecycle: accept -> read -> process -> write -> close. All non-blocking.
</think>

```cpp
#include <sys/epoll.h>
#include <sys/socket.h>
#include <netinet/in.h>
#include <fcntl.h>
#include <unistd.h>
#include <cerrno>
#include <cstring>
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <memory>
#include <functional>

constexpr int MAX_EVENTS = 256;
constexpr size_t INITIAL_BUF_SIZE = 4096;

struct Connection {
    int fd;
    std::vector<char> read_buf;
    std::vector<char> write_buf;
    size_t write_offset = 0;
    bool closing = false;

    explicit Connection(int fd) : fd(fd) {
        read_buf.reserve(INITIAL_BUF_SIZE);
    }

    ~Connection() {
        if (fd >= 0) ::close(fd);
    }

    void queue_write(const char* data, size_t len) {
        write_buf.insert(write_buf.end(), data, data + len);
    }

    bool has_pending_write() const {
        return write_offset < write_buf.size();
    }

    void compact_write_buf() {
        if (write_offset > 0) {
            write_buf.erase(write_buf.begin(),
                           write_buf.begin() + write_offset);
            write_offset = 0;
        }
    }
};

class EpollServer {
public:
    EpollServer(uint16_t port) : port_(port) {}

    ~EpollServer() {
        if (epoll_fd_ >= 0) close(epoll_fd_);
        if (listen_fd_ >= 0) close(listen_fd_);
    }

    bool start() {
        listen_fd_ = socket(AF_INET, SOCK_STREAM | SOCK_NONBLOCK, 0);
        if (listen_fd_ < 0) return perror("socket"), false;

        int opt = 1;
        setsockopt(listen_fd_, SOL_SOCKET, SO_REUSEADDR, &opt, sizeof(opt));
        setsockopt(listen_fd_, SOL_SOCKET, SO_REUSEPORT, &opt, sizeof(opt));

        sockaddr_in addr{};
        addr.sin_family = AF_INET;
        addr.sin_port = htons(port_);
        addr.sin_addr.s_addr = INADDR_ANY;

        if (bind(listen_fd_, (sockaddr*)&addr, sizeof(addr)) < 0)
            return perror("bind"), false;
        if (listen(listen_fd_, SOMAXCONN) < 0)
            return perror("listen"), false;

        epoll_fd_ = epoll_create1(EPOLL_CLOEXEC);
        if (epoll_fd_ < 0) return perror("epoll_create1"), false;

        epoll_event ev{};
        ev.events = EPOLLIN;
        ev.data.fd = listen_fd_;
        epoll_ctl(epoll_fd_, EPOLL_CTL_ADD, listen_fd_, &ev);

        std::cout << "Listening on port " << port_ << std::endl;
        return true;
    }

    void run() {
        epoll_event events[MAX_EVENTS];
        running_ = true;

        while (running_) {
            int n = epoll_wait(epoll_fd_, events, MAX_EVENTS, 1000);
            for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
                if (events[i].data.fd == listen_fd_) {
                    handle_accept();
                } else {
                    int fd = events[i].data.fd;
                    auto it = connections_.find(fd);
                    if (it == connections_.end()) continue;

                    auto& conn = it->second;
                    if (events[i].events & (EPOLLERR | EPOLLHUP)) {
                        close_connection(fd);
                        continue;
                    }
                    if (events[i].events & EPOLLIN) {
                        handle_read(conn);
                    }
                    if (events[i].events & EPOLLOUT) {
                        handle_write(conn);
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }

private:
    void set_nonblocking(int fd) {
        int flags = fcntl(fd, F_GETFL, 0);
        fcntl(fd, F_SETFL, flags | O_NONBLOCK);
    }

    void handle_accept() {
        // Accept all pending connections (edge-triggered on listen socket)
        while (true) {
            sockaddr_in addr{};
            socklen_t len = sizeof(addr);
            int fd = accept4(listen_fd_, (sockaddr*)&addr, &len,
                           SOCK_NONBLOCK | SOCK_CLOEXEC);
            if (fd < 0) {
                if (errno == EAGAIN || errno == EWOULDBLOCK) break;
                perror("accept");
                break;
            }

            epoll_event ev{};
            ev.events = EPOLLIN | EPOLLET; // Edge-triggered
            ev.data.fd = fd;
            epoll_ctl(epoll_fd_, EPOLL_CTL_ADD, fd, &ev);

            connections_.emplace(fd, std::make_unique<Connection>(fd));
        }
    }

    void handle_read(std::unique_ptr<Connection>& conn) {
        // Edge-triggered: must drain completely
        char buf[8192];
        while (true) {
            ssize_t n = read(conn->fd, buf, sizeof(buf));
            if (n > 0) {
                conn->read_buf.insert(conn->read_buf.end(), buf, buf + n);
            } else if (n == 0) {
                close_connection(conn->fd);
                return;
            } else {
                if (errno == EAGAIN || errno == EWOULDBLOCK) break;
                close_connection(conn->fd);
                return;
            }
        }

        // Process complete messages (echo server: echo everything)
        if (!conn->read_buf.empty()) {
            conn->queue_write(conn->read_buf.data(), conn->read_buf.size());
            conn->read_buf.clear();
            handle_write(conn);
        }
    }

    void handle_write(std::unique_ptr<Connection>& conn) {
        while (conn->has_pending_write()) {
            size_t remaining = conn->write_buf.size() - conn->write_offset;
            ssize_t n = write(conn->fd,
                            conn->write_buf.data() + conn->write_offset,
                            remaining);
            if (n > 0) {
                conn->write_offset += n;
            } else if (n < 0) {
                if (errno == EAGAIN || errno == EWOULDBLOCK) {
                    // Enable EPOLLOUT to know when we can write again
                    epoll_event ev{};
                    ev.events = EPOLLIN | EPOLLOUT | EPOLLET;
                    ev.data.fd = conn->fd;
                    epoll_ctl(epoll_fd_, EPOLL_CTL_MOD, conn->fd, &ev);
                    return;
                }
                close_connection(conn->fd);
                return;
            }
        }

        conn->compact_write_buf();
        // Disable EPOLLOUT since we flushed everything
        epoll_event ev{};
        ev.events = EPOLLIN | EPOLLET;
        ev.data.fd = conn->fd;
        epoll_ctl(epoll_fd_, EPOLL_CTL_MOD, conn->fd, &ev);
    }

    void close_connection(int fd) {
        epoll_ctl(epoll_fd_, EPOLL_CTL_DEL, fd, nullptr);
        connections_.erase(fd);
    }

    uint16_t port_;
    int listen_fd_ = -1;
    int epoll_fd_ = -1;
    bool running_ = false;
    std::unordered_map<int, std::unique_ptr<Connection>> connections_;
};

int main() {
    EpollServer server(8080);
    if (server.start()) {
        server.run();
    }
    return 0;
}
```

instruction: Implement a simple HTTP/1.1 protocol parser in C++ that handles chunked transfer encoding, persistent connections, header parsing, and content-length based body reading.
output:
<think>
HTTP/1.1 parsing is deceptively complex. The key challenges: (1) Header parsing - headers are CRLF-delimited, the header section ends with double CRLF. Header names are case-insensitive. Multi-value headers can appear multiple times or be comma-separated. We need to handle folded headers (continuation lines starting with whitespace, though deprecated). (2) Body reading has two modes: Content-Length (read exactly N bytes) and chunked transfer encoding (series of chunks, each prefixed with hex size + CRLF, terminated by 0-size chunk). (3) Persistent connections - HTTP/1.1 defaults to keep-alive, so after reading one response we need to correctly identify where it ends and the next request/response begins. This means we MUST correctly determine body length. (4) The parser should be incremental - we might receive partial data, so it needs to handle being called with incomplete input and resuming. A state machine is the right approach: PARSING_REQUEST_LINE -> PARSING_HEADERS -> PARSING_BODY -> COMPLETE.
</think>

```cpp
#include <string>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <vector>
#include <sstream>
#include <algorithm>
#include <cstdlib>
#include <optional>

enum class ParseState {
    RequestLine,
    Headers,
    Body,
    ChunkedBody,
    ChunkedTrailer,
    Complete,
    Error
};

struct HttpRequest {
    std::string method;
    std::string uri;
    std::string version;
    std::unordered_map<std::string, std::string> headers;
    std::vector<char> body;
    bool keep_alive = true;

    std::string header(const std::string& name) const {
        // Case-insensitive lookup
        std::string lower = name;
        std::transform(lower.begin(), lower.end(), lower.begin(), ::tolower);
        auto it = headers.find(lower);
        return it != headers.end() ? it->second : "";
    }
};

class HttpParser {
public:
    HttpParser() = default;

    // Feed data incrementally. Returns bytes consumed.
    size_t feed(const char* data, size_t len) {
        size_t consumed = 0;
        buffer_.insert(buffer_.end(), data, data + len);

        while (state_ != ParseState::Complete && state_ != ParseState::Error) {
            size_t before = buffer_.size();
            switch (state_) {
            case ParseState::RequestLine:
                parse_request_line();
                break;
            case ParseState::Headers:
                parse_headers();
                break;
            case ParseState::Body:
                parse_body();
                break;
            case ParseState::ChunkedBody:
                parse_chunked();
                break;
            case ParseState::ChunkedTrailer:
                parse_chunked_trailer();
                break;
            default:
                break;
            }
            if (buffer_.size() == before && state_ != ParseState::Complete) {
                break; // No progress, need more data
            }
            consumed += (before - buffer_.size());
        }
        return consumed;
    }

    bool is_complete() const { return state_ == ParseState::Complete; }
    bool has_error() const { return state_ == ParseState::Error; }
    const HttpRequest& request() const { return request_; }

    void reset() {
        state_ = ParseState::RequestLine;
        request_ = HttpRequest{};
        buffer_.clear();
        content_length_ = 0;
        body_read_ = 0;
        chunk_remaining_ = 0;
    }

private:
    std::optional<std::string> read_line() {
        auto it = std::search(buffer_.begin(), buffer_.end(),
                             "\r\n", "\r\n" + 2);
        if (it == buffer_.end()) return std::nullopt;
        std::string line(buffer_.begin(), it);
        buffer_.erase(buffer_.begin(), it + 2);
        return line;
    }

    void parse_request_line() {
        auto line = read_line();
        if (!line) return;

        std::istringstream iss(*line);
        if (!(iss >> request_.method >> request_.uri >> request_.version)) {
            state_ = ParseState::Error;
            return;
        }
        state_ = ParseState::Headers;
    }

    void parse_headers() {
        while (true) {
            auto line = read_line();
            if (!line) return;

            if (line->empty()) {
                // End of headers
                finalize_headers();
                return;
            }

            auto colon = line->find(':');
            if (colon == std::string::npos) {
                state_ = ParseState::Error;
                return;
            }

            std::string name = line->substr(0, colon);
            std::string value = line->substr(colon + 1);

            // Trim whitespace
            while (!value.empty() && value[0] == ' ') value.erase(0, 1);
            while (!value.empty() && value.back() == ' ') value.pop_back();

            // Store lowercase
            std::transform(name.begin(), name.end(), name.begin(), ::tolower);

            auto it = request_.headers.find(name);
            if (it != request_.headers.end()) {
                it->second += ", " + value;
            } else {
                request_.headers[name] = value;
            }
        }
    }

    void finalize_headers() {
        // Determine body handling
        auto te = request_.header("transfer-encoding");
        if (te.find("chunked") != std::string::npos) {
            state_ = ParseState::ChunkedBody;
            return;
        }

        auto cl = request_.header("content-length");
        if (!cl.empty()) {
            content_length_ = std::stoul(cl);
            if (content_length_ > 0) {
                request_.body.reserve(content_length_);
                state_ = ParseState::Body;
            } else {
                state_ = ParseState::Complete;
            }
            return;
        }

        // No body
        auto conn = request_.header("connection");
        request_.keep_alive = (request_.version == "HTTP/1.1" && conn != "close")
                            || conn == "keep-alive";
        state_ = ParseState::Complete;
    }

    void parse_body() {
        size_t need = content_length_ - body_read_;
        size_t available = std::min(need, buffer_.size());

        request_.body.insert(request_.body.end(),
                           buffer_.begin(), buffer_.begin() + available);
        buffer_.erase(buffer_.begin(), buffer_.begin() + available);
        body_read_ += available;

        if (body_read_ >= content_length_) {
            state_ = ParseState::Complete;
        }
    }

    void parse_chunked() {
        while (true) {
            if (chunk_remaining_ > 0) {
                size_t available = std::min(chunk_remaining_, buffer_.size());
                request_.body.insert(request_.body.end(),
                                   buffer_.begin(), buffer_.begin() + available);
                buffer_.erase(buffer_.begin(), buffer_.begin() + available);
                chunk_remaining_ -= available;

                if (chunk_remaining_ > 0) return; // Need more data

                // Consume trailing CRLF after chunk data
                if (buffer_.size() < 2) return;
                buffer_.erase(buffer_.begin(), buffer_.begin() + 2);
            }

            // Read chunk size line
            auto line = read_line();
            if (!line) return;

            // Parse hex size (ignore extensions after semicolon)
            auto semi = line->find(';');
            std::string hex_str = (semi != std::string::npos)
                ? line->substr(0, semi) : *line;
            chunk_remaining_ = std::stoul(hex_str, nullptr, 16);

            if (chunk_remaining_ == 0) {
                state_ = ParseState::ChunkedTrailer;
                return;
            }
        }
    }

    void parse_chunked_trailer() {
        // Read and discard trailer headers until empty line
        while (true) {
            auto line = read_line();
            if (!line) return;
            if (line->empty()) {
                state_ = ParseState::Complete;
                return;
            }
        }
    }

    ParseState state_ = ParseState::RequestLine;
    HttpRequest request_;
    std::vector<char> buffer_;
    size_t content_length_ = 0;
    size_t body_read_ = 0;
    size_t chunk_remaining_ = 0;
};

// Usage example
int main() {
    HttpParser parser;

    const char* raw =
        "POST /api/data HTTP/1.1\r\n"
        "Host: example.com\r\n"
        "Transfer-Encoding: chunked\r\n"
        "Content-Type: application/json\r\n"
        "\r\n"
        "7\r\n"
        "{\"a\":1}\r\n"
        "0\r\n"
        "\r\n";

    parser.feed(raw, strlen(raw));

    if (parser.is_complete()) {
        auto& req = parser.request();
        std::cout << req.method << " " << req.uri << std::endl;
        std::cout << "Body: " << std::string(req.body.begin(), req.body.end()) << std::endl;
    }
    return 0;
}
```

instruction: Build a C++ implementation of a custom binary protocol over TCP using Boost.Asio with message framing, serialization, versioning, and error recovery.
output:
<think>
Custom binary protocols need careful design for real-world use. Key components: (1) Message framing - how do we know where one message ends and the next begins? Common approaches: length-prefix (4 bytes length + payload) or delimiter-based. Length-prefix is better for binary protocols because delimiters require escaping in binary data. (2) Versioning - the header should include a protocol version so old clients can talk to new servers with graceful degradation. (3) Serialization - we need to convert structs to bytes and back. Manual serialization with fixed-size fields gives best performance and control. We should handle endianness explicitly (network byte order = big endian). (4) Error recovery - if we receive corrupt data, we need to resynchronize. Options: magic bytes at the start of each frame (allows scanning for valid frame boundaries), or connection reset. (5) The wire format: [magic: 2 bytes][version: 1 byte][type: 1 byte][length: 4 bytes][payload: N bytes][checksum: 4 bytes]. CRC32 for the checksum detects corruption.
</think>

```cpp
#include <boost/asio.hpp>
#include <boost/crc.hpp>
#include <cstdint>
#include <cstring>
#include <iostream>
#include <memory>
#include <vector>
#include <functional>

namespace asio = boost::asio;
using tcp = asio::ip::tcp;

// Wire format constants
constexpr uint16_t MAGIC = 0xBEEF;
constexpr uint8_t PROTOCOL_VERSION = 1;
constexpr size_t HEADER_SIZE = 8; // magic(2) + version(1) + type(1) + length(4)
constexpr size_t CHECKSUM_SIZE = 4;
constexpr size_t MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE = 1 * 1024 * 1024; // 1MB max

enum class MessageType : uint8_t {
    Heartbeat = 0,
    Request = 1,
    Response = 2,
    Error = 3,
};

struct FrameHeader {
    uint16_t magic;
    uint8_t version;
    MessageType type;
    uint32_t payload_length;
};

// Endian-safe serialization
class ByteWriter {
public:
    void write_u8(uint8_t v) { buf_.push_back(v); }
    void write_u16(uint16_t v) {
        buf_.push_back((v >> 8) & 0xFF);
        buf_.push_back(v & 0xFF);
    }
    void write_u32(uint32_t v) {
        buf_.push_back((v >> 24) & 0xFF);
        buf_.push_back((v >> 16) & 0xFF);
        buf_.push_back((v >> 8) & 0xFF);
        buf_.push_back(v & 0xFF);
    }
    void write_string(const std::string& s) {
        write_u32(static_cast<uint32_t>(s.size()));
        buf_.insert(buf_.end(), s.begin(), s.end());
    }
    void write_bytes(const void* data, size_t len) {
        auto p = static_cast<const uint8_t*>(data);
        buf_.insert(buf_.end(), p, p + len);
    }
    const std::vector<uint8_t>& data() const { return buf_; }
    size_t size() const { return buf_.size(); }
private:
    std::vector<uint8_t> buf_;
};

class ByteReader {
public:
    ByteReader(const uint8_t* data, size_t len) : data_(data), len_(len) {}
    bool read_u8(uint8_t& v) {
        if (pos_ + 1 > len_) return false;
        v = data_[pos_++];
        return true;
    }
    bool read_u16(uint16_t& v) {
        if (pos_ + 2 > len_) return false;
        v = (uint16_t(data_[pos_]) << 8) | data_[pos_ + 1];
        pos_ += 2;
        return true;
    }
    bool read_u32(uint32_t& v) {
        if (pos_ + 4 > len_) return false;
        v = (uint32_t(data_[pos_]) << 24) | (uint32_t(data_[pos_+1]) << 16)
          | (uint32_t(data_[pos_+2]) << 8) | data_[pos_+3];
        pos_ += 4;
        return true;
    }
    bool read_string(std::string& s) {
        uint32_t len;
        if (!read_u32(len)) return false;
        if (pos_ + len > len_) return false;
        s.assign(data_ + pos_, data_ + pos_ + len);
        pos_ += len;
        return true;
    }
    size_t remaining() const { return len_ - pos_; }
private:
    const uint8_t* data_;
    size_t len_;
    size_t pos_ = 0;
};

uint32_t compute_crc32(const uint8_t* data, size_t len) {
    boost::crc_32_type crc;
    crc.process_bytes(data, len);
    return crc.checksum();
}

class FrameCodec {
public:
    // Encode a message into a framed wire format
    static std::vector<uint8_t> encode(
        MessageType type, const std::vector<uint8_t>& payload
    ) {
        ByteWriter w;
        // Header
        w.write_u16(MAGIC);
        w.write_u8(PROTOCOL_VERSION);
        w.write_u8(static_cast<uint8_t>(type));
        w.write_u32(static_cast<uint32_t>(payload.size()));
        // Payload
        w.write_bytes(payload.data(), payload.size());
        // CRC32 over header + payload
        uint32_t crc = compute_crc32(w.data().data(), w.size());
        w.write_u32(crc);
        return w.data();
    }

    // Decode header from buffer. Returns nullopt if incomplete.
    static std::optional<FrameHeader> decode_header(const uint8_t* data, size_t len) {
        if (len < HEADER_SIZE) return std::nullopt;
        ByteReader r(data, len);
        FrameHeader h{};
        r.read_u16(h.magic);
        r.read_u8(h.version);
        uint8_t type_raw;
        r.read_u8(type_raw);
        h.type = static_cast<MessageType>(type_raw);
        r.read_u32(h.payload_length);
        return h;
    }

    // Validate a complete frame
    enum class ValidateResult { Ok, BadMagic, BadVersion, BadChecksum, TooLarge };

    static ValidateResult validate(
        const uint8_t* data, size_t frame_len, const FrameHeader& header
    ) {
        if (header.magic != MAGIC) return ValidateResult::BadMagic;
        if (header.version > PROTOCOL_VERSION) return ValidateResult::BadVersion;
        if (header.payload_length > MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE) return ValidateResult::TooLarge;

        size_t data_len = HEADER_SIZE + header.payload_length;
        uint32_t expected_crc = compute_crc32(data, data_len);
        ByteReader r(data + data_len, CHECKSUM_SIZE);
        uint32_t actual_crc;
        r.read_u32(actual_crc);

        if (expected_crc != actual_crc) return ValidateResult::BadChecksum;
        return ValidateResult::Ok;
    }

    static size_t frame_size(const FrameHeader& h) {
        return HEADER_SIZE + h.payload_length + CHECKSUM_SIZE;
    }
};

// Connection that reads/writes framed messages
class FramedConnection : public std::enable_shared_from_this<FramedConnection> {
public:
    using MessageHandler = std::function<void(MessageType, std::vector<uint8_t>)>;

    FramedConnection(tcp::socket socket, MessageHandler handler)
        : socket_(std::move(socket)), handler_(std::move(handler)) {
        read_buf_.resize(8192);
    }

    void start() { do_read(); }

    void send(MessageType type, const std::vector<uint8_t>& payload) {
        auto frame = FrameCodec::encode(type, payload);
        auto self = shared_from_this();
        asio::async_write(socket_, asio::buffer(frame),
            [self](auto ec, auto) {
                if (ec) std::cerr << "Write error: " << ec.message() << std::endl;
            });
    }

private:
    void do_read() {
        auto self = shared_from_this();
        socket_.async_read_some(asio::buffer(read_buf_.data() + buffered_, read_buf_.size() - buffered_),
            [this, self](boost::system::error_code ec, size_t n) {
                if (ec) return;
                buffered_ += n;
                process_buffer();
                if (buffered_ >= read_buf_.size() * 3 / 4) {
                    read_buf_.resize(read_buf_.size() * 2);
                }
                do_read();
            });
    }

    void process_buffer() {
        while (buffered_ >= HEADER_SIZE) {
            auto header = FrameCodec::decode_header(read_buf_.data(), buffered_);
            if (!header) break;

            size_t total = FrameCodec::frame_size(*header);
            if (buffered_ < total) break;

            auto result = FrameCodec::validate(read_buf_.data(), total, *header);
            if (result == FrameCodec::ValidateResult::Ok) {
                std::vector<uint8_t> payload(
                    read_buf_.begin() + HEADER_SIZE,
                    read_buf_.begin() + HEADER_SIZE + header->payload_length);
                handler_(header->type, std::move(payload));
            } else {
                std::cerr << "Frame validation failed, scanning for next magic" << std::endl;
                // Error recovery: scan for next magic byte sequence
                total = scan_for_magic(1);
            }

            // Consume processed bytes
            std::memmove(read_buf_.data(), read_buf_.data() + total, buffered_ - total);
            buffered_ -= total;
        }
    }

    size_t scan_for_magic(size_t start_offset) {
        for (size_t i = start_offset; i + 1 < buffered_; ++i) {
            uint16_t candidate = (uint16_t(read_buf_[i]) << 8) | read_buf_[i + 1];
            if (candidate == MAGIC) return i;
        }
        return buffered_; // Skip everything
    }

    tcp::socket socket_;
    MessageHandler handler_;
    std::vector<uint8_t> read_buf_;
    size_t buffered_ = 0;
};
```
